title: Allow Desktop App to "close" to Windows SysTray
excerpt: When I click “X” in the top right corner of the Desktop App, the app closes…  Add an option to minimize to systemtray when minimized or “X” is clicked.  i.e. Microsoft Teams has a setting, “On close, keep the application…

posts.author: ITBill (Bill Moller)
posts.cookedHtml: When I click “X” in the top right corner of the Desktop App, the app closes… Add an option to minimize to systemtray when minimized or “X” is clicked. i.e. Microsoft Teams has a setting, “On close, keep the application running” which, when checked, sends Teams to the systray.

posts.author: adamb (Adam Brown)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes. Please! And have an option to start with Windows. Start automatically in the tray. Click the tray icon or the desktop/start menu app to open. The “X” closes/minimizes to the tray. Combining these would allow Monday notifications to start flowing as soon as a user logs in without them having to remember to open it and continue running in the background without taking up all your desktop space or having to run minimized. Almost all other major communications systems do this. Teams, Slack, Zoom. Let’s go Monday.
